meta.siteDescription: Department of Biological Sciences, CENUR Litoral Norte, Universidad de la República, Uruguay.

hero.eyebrow: CENUR Litoral Norte · Universidad de la República
hero.title: Department of Biological Sciences
hero.subtitle: We generate and share knowledge in biology through research, teaching, and outreach, based at the Salto and Paysandú campuses of Uruguay's Litoral Norte region.

about.title: About the Department
about.intro: The Department of Biological Sciences (DCB) was established in June 2014 to bring together faculty and researchers in the biological sciences based at the Salto and Paysandú campuses of CENUR Litoral Norte, Universidad de la República. It now comprises roughly 55 members across 10 research groups, together with the coordination team of the Biology-Biochemistry Cycle (CBB) and the Salto campus Laboratory Support Unit.
about.mission: To work jointly across the broad field of Biology, carrying out coordinated research, teaching, and outreach activities at CENUR Litoral Norte, in cooperation with other university units across the region and the country.
about.infrastructure: Next to the main building of the Salto campus, an 1,800 m² research platform houses 8 of the Department's 11 laboratories, including a biosafety level 3 (P3) facility — the only one of its kind in Uruguay.
values: Research in biophysical chemistry, molecular biology, ecology, and applied biology
Teaching of the Biology-Biochemistry Cycle (CBB) and participation in other CENUR Litoral Norte programs
University outreach and engagement with the region's productive and social communities
Cooperation with other units of Universidad de la República, in particular CEINBIO and Facultad de Ciencias

teaching.title: Teaching
teaching.subtitle: Biology-Biochemistry Cycle (CBB) and the Bachelor's in Human Biology.
teaching.intro: The Department runs the Biology-Biochemistry Cycle (CBB), approved by UdelaR's CDC in 2012 and launched in 2018, and also contributes to the Bachelor's in Human Biology and to undergraduate, graduate, and continuing-education courses across the university.
teaching.cbbDescription: Provides foundational training in biological sciences and biochemistry at the Salto and Paysandú campuses, taught by faculty from the Department's research groups. It is equivalent to 90 credits of first-year university study, recognized by Facultad de Ciencias toward the Bachelor's degrees in Biological Sciences and in Biochemistry, and regionally toward the Bachelor's in Human Biology.
cbbAreas: Methodological and General Training (5 to 20 credits)
teaching.note: This section is under construction: the full curriculum and graduate offerings will be added soon.
